key_insight: Tensorion generalizes Muon from matrices to higher-order tensors by optimizing with a tractable linear minimization oracle over a tensor norm ball that closely controls the tensor spectral norm.
engineering_relevance: It offers a way to respect multilinear weight structure in tensor-valued models, potentially improving convergence stability over vectorized Adam-style updates.
method: Define a tensor norm that balances spectral-norm tightness with LMO tractability.
Compute the LMO via operations on adaptively chosen unfolding matrices.
Reduce exactly to Muon when the tensor order is 2 (matrix case).
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on tensor-based computer vision tasks, where Tensorion showed improved convergence behavior and more stable updates versus Adam-based and other tensor-aware baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on the chosen unfolding strategy and the cost of solving the LMO may limit practicality for very large tensors.
paper_title: Tensorion: A Tensor-Aware Generalization of the Muon Optimizer
